description: In SQL, run the package-local gate, fix the first owned diagnostic, rerun, then broaden.
body: In SQL, the fastest path through errors is to let EXPLAIN on the statement to confirm it parses and the planner accepts it and EXPLAIN ANALYZE, the query plan, and index-usage inspection drive one fix at a time rather than guessing. Use when the migration applied against a scratch database (the same engine/version as prod) or the analyzers report multiple diagnostics. Do not batch speculative edits across files, and avoid suppressing diagnostics to move on. Actions: inspect the package, run the gate, fix only the engine's error code and the offending clause it points to, rerun a transaction that runs the statement, asserts row counts, then rolls back, then widen to the migration applied against a scratch database (the same engine/version as prod) for the whole package. Validation signals include a shrinking diagnostic count, a passing focused test, and a clean package-wide gate. Risk boundary: if a diagnostic implies a design or API change, stop and escalate instead of forcing the gate green.
